title: senecio qosqoensis
intro: Senecio qosqoensis, also known as Queñua or Peruvian groundsel, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family native to the Andean highlands of Peru. It has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and aiding in the treatment of wounds and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, Senecio qosqoensis is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
